verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically anchored to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, placing it on the cytoplasmic face — not the extracellular face. Under baseline conditions it is inner-leaflet-anchored and inaccessible to extracellular binders targeting the protein body. However, multiple cancer-cell ecto-SRC reports document SRC kinase domain exposure on the outer leaflet of tumor cells (cancer ecto-kinase phenomenon), qualifying as cell_state_induced / oncogenic transformation. Additionally, SRC participates in regulated exocytosis and can associate with surface receptor complexes during activation. The canonical biology is definitively inner-leaflet, but the documented cancer-cell ecto-pool means `no` would be an overstatement. `contextual` / `cell_state_induced` is the correct call.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Extent and wash-resistance of the reported cancer-cell ecto-SRC pool is debated; it is unclear whether surface display is robust enough to support antibody-based targeting of the protein body on tumor cells.
